name: argos-monitoring

metadata.name: argos-monitoring
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU Affero General Public License v3 or later (AGPLv3+)

documentation:
# Argos monitoring

A monitoring and status board for your websites.

![Screenshot of Argos’ status page](docs/dashboard.jpg)

1. Define a list of websites to monitor
2. Specify a list of checks to run on these websites.
3. Argos will run the checks periodically and alert you if something goes wrong.

Internally, a HTTP API is exposed, and a job queue is used to distribute the checks to the agents.

- [Online documentation](https://argos-monitoring.framasoft.org/)
- [Issue tracker](https://framagit.org/framasoft/framaspace/argos/-/issues)

## Requirements

- **Python**: 3.11+
- **Backends**: SQLite (development), PostgreSQL 14+ (production)

functions.name: argos.server.migrations.env.run_migrations_offline
documentation:
Run migrations in 'offline' mode.

This configures the context with just a URL
and not an Engine, though an Engine is acceptable
here as well.  By skipping the Engine creation
we don't even need a DBAPI to be available.

Calls to context.execute() here emit the given string to the
script output.

functions.name: argos.server.migrations.env.run_migrations_online
documentation:
Run migrations in 'online' mode.

In this scenario we need to create an Engine
and associate a connection with the context.

functions.name: argos.commands.agent
documentation:
Get and run tasks for the provided server. Will wait for new tasks.

Usage: argos agent https://argos.example.org "auth-token-here"

Alternatively, you can use the following environment variables to avoid passing
arguments to the agent on the command line:

   
  ARGOS_AGENT_SERVER_URL=https://argos.example.org
  ARGOS_AGENT_TOKEN=auth-token-here

functions.name: argos.commands.start
documentation:
Starts the server (use only for testing or development!)

See https://argos-monitoring.framasoft.org/deployment/systemd.html#server
for advices on how to start the server for production.

functions.name: argos.commands.cleandb
documentation:
Clean the database (to run routinely)

 
- Removes old results from the database.
- Removes locks from tasks that have been locked for too long.

functions.name: argos.commands.watch_agents
documentation:
Watch agents (to run routinely)

Issues a warning if no agent has been seen by the server for a given time.

methods.name: argos.server.models.Task.last_result
documentation: Get last result of the task

methods.name: argos.server.models.Task.status
documentation: Get status of the task, i.e. the status of its last result

There is one task per check.

It contains all information needed to run the jobs on the agents.
Agents will return information in the result table.

classes.name: argos.server.models.Result
documentation:
There are multiple results per task.

The results store information returned by the agents.

You can read `status` as  "Was the agent able to do the check?"
while the `severity` depends on the return value of the check.

classes.name: argos.server.models.ConfigCache
documentation:
Database model containing information on the current state
of the configuration.

This is used to determine if tasks are to be updated.

These settings are cached:
- general_frequency: the content of general.frequency setting, in minutes
                     ex: 5
- websites_hash:     the hash (sha256sum) of websites setting, to allow a quick
                     comparison without looping through all websites.
                     ex: 8b886e7db7b553fe99f6d5437f31745987e243c77b2109b84cf9a7f8bf7d75b1

documentation: Database model for user authentication

XXX We could name this Result, but is it could overlap with schemas.Result.
Need to better define the naming around this.
Status can be "Success" / "Failure" / "Error" or "On Check"

documentation: Ensure that the response has a status and return a Response

methods.name: argos.checks.base.BaseCheck.finalize
documentation:
By default, the finalize considers that :

- All FAILUREs should be reported as CRITICAL
- All SUCCESS should be reported as OK
- All ERRORS should be reported as UNKNOWN.

This behaviour can be changed in each check, by defining the `finalize` method.
XXX Allow this to be tweaked by the config.

class_variables.name: argos.checks.base.BaseCheck._registry
documentation: type: ignore[var-annotated]

classes.name: argos.checks.checks.HTTPStatus
documentation: Checks that the HTTP status code is the expected one.

classes.name: argos.checks.checks.HTTPStatusIn
documentation: Checks that the HTTP status code is in the list of expected values.

classes.name: argos.checks.checks.HTTPToHTTPS
documentation: Checks that the HTTP to HTTPS redirection status code is the expected one.

classes.name: argos.checks.checks.HTTPHeadersContain
documentation:
Checks that response headers contains the expected headers
(without checking their values)

classes.name: argos.checks.checks.HTTPHeadersHave
documentation: Checks that response headers contains the expected headers and values

classes.name: argos.checks.checks.HTTPHeadersLike
documentation:
Checks that response headers contains the expected headers and that the values
matches the provided regexes

classes.name: argos.checks.checks.HTTPBodyContains
documentation: Checks that the HTTP body contains the expected string.

classes.name: argos.checks.checks.HTTPBodyLike
documentation: Checks that the HTTP body matches the provided regex.

classes.name: argos.checks.checks.HTTPJsonContains
documentation:
Checks that JSON response contains the expected structure
(without checking the value)

classes.name: argos.checks.checks.HTTPJsonHas
documentation: Checks that JSON response contains the expected structure and values

classes.name: argos.checks.checks.HTTPJsonLike
documentation:
Checks that JSON response contains the expected structure and that the values
matches the provided regexes

classes.name: argos.checks.checks.HTTPJsonIs
documentation: Checks that JSON response is the exact expected JSON object

classes.name: argos.checks.checks.SSLCertificateExpiration

documentation: Returns the number of days in which the certificate will expire.
Checks that the SSL certificate will not expire soon.
